sections.paragraph:
Σαμαρῖτις, ιδος, ἡ (also Σαμαρεῖτις; 1 Macc 10:30; 11:28; EpArist 107; Jos., Bell. 3, 48 al.; IG III, 2892) fem. of Σαμαρίτης adj. and subst., Samaritan ἡ γυνὴ ἡ Σαμαρῖτις the Samaritan woman J 4:9a; cp. 9b.—TW.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

IG IG = Inscriptiones Graecae—List 3

fem. fem. = feminine

adj. adj. = adjective

subst. subst. = substantive(ly)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 912
